current_state: The seven Schoolwide Learner Outcomes — Bilingual Communicators, Compassionate, Confident, Engaged, Innovative, Reflective, and Responsible — are posted as labels at Chiway-Repton School Xiamen, but only Confident carries a published school-authored behavioral descriptor. The other six function as single-word headings without shared definition. Unit plans across the AAO - Academic Affairs Office's departmental roster — Math, English, Humanities, Sciences, Art Department, Business and Economics, Music and Sports Department — do not consistently name which SLOs each unit advances, and assessment artifacts are not tagged to SLOs. PLC cycles run by the Curriculum and Teaching division open and close on subject-content frames rather than SLO frames. Parent reporting at each cycle carries subject grades without an SLO-organized narrative section. Student self-reflection at term reporting and portfolio review rarely cites an SLO as the interpretive lens. The SAO - Student Affairs Office (Pastoral and Well-Being) operates pastoral routines through Homeroom Teachers and the IG1, IG2, AS, and A2 Year Leaders without a shared SLO vocabulary linking those routines to academic evidence. The College Counseling Department, Library, and Future Scholar Innovation Center each run programs that touch SLO territory without naming the SLOs they advance.
desired_state: Each of the seven SLOs carries a published school-authored behavioral descriptor at the depth of the current Confident descriptor, authored by cross-divisional faculty teams drawn from Math, English, Humanities, Sciences, Art Department, Business and Economics, and Music and Sports Department, validated by the Director of Teaching and Curriculum Center and the IHS Principal, and posted in the staff handbook, the parent handbook, and student-facing materials by 2025-11-28. By 2026-02-13, the unit-planning template and gradebook operated through the AAO - Academic Affairs Office and the Curriculum and Teaching division are updated so every department's Term 2 unit plans name one or two SLOs per unit and carry at least one SLO-tagged assessment artifact, and PLC cycles in every academic division open on an SLO frame and close on SLO-tagged evidence recorded in PLC minutes. By 2026-05-15, term reporting and portfolio-review routines deliver an SLO-organized parent narrative for every enrolled student across IG1, IG2, AS, and A2, and an SLO self-reflection from every student, with Homeroom Teachers and Year Leaders in the SAO - Student Affairs Office (Pastoral and Well-Being) facilitating the student reflection step. By 2026-07-24, the annual self-study evidence binder against WASC standards A1, A4, B1, B2, B3, and C1 is compiled with the seven SLOs as the organizing evidence categories, drawing on a full year of unit-level, PLC-level, student-reflection, and parent-reporting artifacts.
rationale: The SLOs are the school's operational expression of its three mission areas: Academic excellence, Caring and safe community, and Global citizenship. Six of seven SLOs lack a shared behavioral descriptor, so faculty, students, parents, and the Parent Committee do not share a common definition of what Bilingual Communicators, Compassionate, Engaged, Innovative, Reflective, or Responsible looks like in daily practice. This gap weakens evidence under WASC standards A1 (vision and mission collectively understood and implemented), A4 (qualitative and quantitative data driving improvement), B1 (curriculum standards-aligned and responsive to student needs), B2 (instruction grounded in research-based strategies producing student agency), B3 (varied assessments measuring student progress), and C1 (school culture of shared responsibility supporting academic and social-emotional growth). The plan addresses the gap across seven areas for improvement — Bilingual Environment carries the Bilingual Communicators work through the English and Humanities departments; Curriculum and Learning carries unit-plan SLO naming and SLO-tagged assessment artifacts across every department; Faculty PD and PLC carries the descriptor authoring and PLC redesign through the Curriculum and Teaching division; School Culture carries Compassionate, Responsible, and Engaged as lived norms through the SAO - Student Affairs Office (Pastoral and Well-Being); Student Data and Well-being carries SLO-tagged evidence in PLC data cycles and term-level student self-reflection; Community Involvement carries SLO-organized parent reporting; Resources and Tools carries the unit-planning template and gradebook updates the rest of the work depends on.
student_impact_framing: Students at Chiway-Repton School Xiamen will encounter the SLOs as named instructional and reflective vocabulary rather than wall posters. Unit plans in every department will tell students which one or two SLOs each unit advances, and at least one assessment per unit will produce SLO-tagged evidence the student can review. At term reporting and portfolio review, every student across IG1, IG2, AS, and A2 will produce an SLO self-reflection naming which SLOs a given piece of their work demonstrates, facilitated by Homeroom Teachers under Year Leaders in the SAO - Student Affairs Office (Pastoral and Well-Being). Bilingual Communicators work in English and Humanities will give students a shared definition of bilingual proficiency tied to the school's vision clause on effective communication in Chinese and English. Reflective and Confident work will give students named language for self-assurance and reflective practice. Compassionate, Responsible, and Engaged work led through the SAO - Student Affairs Office (Pastoral and Well-Being) will give students shared norms for behavior in homerooms, classrooms, and co-curricular settings. Parent reporting carrying an SLO-organized narrative section means each student's family receives, at each reporting cycle, a named account of growth across all seven SLOs in addition to subject grades.
provenance: The plan draws on the existing published Confident SLO descriptor as the depth-benchmark for authoring descriptors for the other six SLOs. It draws on the school's vision clauses on engaging and realizing academic potential, lifelong learning, personal responsibility, self-esteem, communicating ideas in Chinese and English, and compassion for others, and on the mission clauses GROUNDED, ROUNDED, and UNBOUNDED. It draws on the AAO - Academic Affairs Office's existing unit-plan repository and gradebook, the Curriculum and Teaching division's existing PLC cycle, the SAO - Student Affairs Office (Pastoral and Well-Being)'s existing homeroom and Year Leader routines across IG1, IG2, AS, and A2, the existing reporting-cycle template, and the existing portfolio-review routine. Evidence sources for the 2026-07-24 self-study binder are the year's unit plans, PLC minutes, SLO-tagged assessment artifacts, student self-reflections, parent reporting narratives, and classroom walkthrough notes, organized against WASC standards A1, A4, B1, B2, B3, and C1. Stakeholder consultation under the Policy revision (6-step consultative) method engages Administration / Leadership, Staff / Faculty, Students, Parents, and the Parent Committee.
